records.ety: [AS. hwettan; akin to D. wetten, G. wetzen, OHG. wezzen, Icel. hvetja, Sw. vättja, and AS. hwæt vigorous, brave, OS. hwat, OHG. waz, was, sharp, Icel. hvatr, bold, active, Sw. hvass sharp, Dan. hvas, Goth. hwassaba sharply, and probably to Skr. cud to impel, urge on.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Whetted; p. pr. & vb. n. Whetting.]

defs: To rub or on with some substance, as a piece of stone, for the purpose of sharpening; to sharpen by attrition; as, to whet a knife.
quotes: Here roams the wolf, the eagle whets his beak.

defs: To make sharp, keen, or eager; to excite; to stimulate; as, to whet the appetite or the courage.
quotes: Since Cassius first did whet me against Caesar, I have not slept.

defs: That which whets or sharpens; esp., an appetizer.
